evidence_quotes.quote: One example is The Crew (2014) a game I purchased and installed which became completely unplayable after its servers were shut down.
Once support ends, access disappears forever.
It is unacceptable that games can be removed without offering offline functionality or other preservation methods.

paragraphs.text: Dear Commission, I am writing to express my support for the Stop Killing Games initiative. As a regular buyer of video games, Ive experienced firsthand the negative effects of publishers shutting down games without offering alternatives for continued access. One example is The Crew (2014) a game I purchased and installed which became completely unplayable after its servers were shut down. Im also concerned about other titles like Need for Speed (2015), which rely entirely on online connectivity. Once support ends, access disappears forever. Consumers should have the right to use what theyve paid for, even after official support ends. It is unacceptable that games can be removed without offering offline functionality or other preservation methods. I urge the European Union to take steps to protect digital consumers in the gaming industry and prevent this from becoming a standard practice. Sincerely, A concerned non-EU European player
